claim: BTC is more likely than not to resolve Yes, but the swarm’s apparent large edge versus the 0.71 assignment snapshot is only partially independently verified because all strong evidence still funnels through the same Binance minute-close mechanism and the decisive noon ET candle had not yet occurred at research time. My synthesis compresses the swarm modestly toward market while preserving a clear Yes lean.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: practical retrieval/alignment of the exact Binance 12:00 ET 1m close remains slightly fragile pre-settlement, though wording is clear
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules point to Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close as source of truth
Independent synthesis-stage Binance spot check still showed BTC/USDT around 71451, comfortably above 70000 before settlement
Recent Binance 1-minute candles remained above 70000 during the synthesis check
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: The exact governing 12:00 ET Binance candle close was not yet independently captured, so final path risk remains unverified.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A brief late selloff of roughly 2% into the exact noon ET Binance minute could still resolve No despite the pre-noon cushion.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mainly about how much probability mass to assign to exact-minute path risk versus short-horizon price persistence.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Resolve Yes only if the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle’s final close is strictly above 70000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: The decisive evidence is the exact Binance 12:00 ET minute close, and pre-noon price can still move materially before then.
decision_blockers: No independent capture yet of the exact governing settlement candle
Assignment market snapshot may be stale relative to later market repricing
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Directly verify the Binance 16:00 UTC / 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close at or immediately after settlement.

sections.Alpha summary: Market-implied baseline was 0.71. My post-synthesis range is 0.76 to 0.86 Yes. That still suggests a positive edge versus the assignment snapshot, but it is only moderately actionable because the contract is a single-minute Binance close and the swarm’s larger apparent edge was not verified strongly enough to trust at full size. The likely mispricing, if any, is market underweighting of short-horizon persistence while BTC was already materially above the threshold.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No persona was missing. I relied mainly on the raw persona findings and used the sidecars as navigation aids; the sidecars looked broadly faithful rather than distorted. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were not needed beyond what was already embedded in the raw findings. Coverage is complete because the full intended persona set was present and coherent, though not all lanes were equally discriminating.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The runtime baseline to synthesize against is 0.71. Several personas also observed a later public Polymarket reading around the mid-90s, implying the assignment snapshot may have been stale or taken earlier in the move. I treat 0.71 as the official comparison point but downweight any huge claimed edge because live market movement likely narrowed it during the swarm run.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My own final estimate is 0.76 to 0.86 Yes. This preserves the swarm’s clear Yes lean while trimming the top end because the decisive contract event had not yet happened and the strongest evidence remained pre-settlement spot checks rather than direct capture of the governing candle.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: The swarm center was around 0.82 and the swarm range 0.78 to 0.90. My range is slightly compressed and a bit more cautious on the high end, not because the swarm was directionally wrong, but because a roughly 11-point edge versus the assignment market triggers a higher verification bar. Independent synthesis-stage checking confirmed BTC still comfortably above 70k, but it did not independently verify the exact settlement candle or justify full confidence near the most bullish lane.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: Against the assignment snapshot, I disagree modestly in a Yes direction. Against the likely later live market, I probably roughly agree. The key point is that the apparent edge is sensitive to timestamp: versus 0.71 there is still upside, but versus a later repriced market in the 90s there may be little or no edge left. That makes this more a question of stale baseline versus live decision timing than deep analytical disagreement.
sections.Independent verification of edge: I performed a bounded synthesis-stage truth-finding pass focused on the highest-yield checks: re-reading the raw lanes for consistency and independently checking Binance spot plus recent 1-minute candles. This verified that BTC/USDT was still around 71,451 and that recent minute closes remained above 70,000. That is meaningful because it confirms the above-threshold regime persisted after the persona snapshots. Verification quality is medium, not high, because it still depends on the same settlement venue and does not include the exact noon ET candle close.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: Yes. I compressed modestly toward market because the swarm’s more aggressive upside over the 0.71 baseline could not be independently verified strongly enough to support the highest lane estimates with confidence. What was missing was direct verification of the exact governing candle and stronger independent evidence about last-hour downside tail risk. That kept me from endorsing the 0.90 view despite agreeing that Yes was favored.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The only catalyst that really matters is the Binance 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC 1-minute close itself. Before that, the edge tends to decay into pure path risk: as time shortens while price remains above 70k, Yes should strengthen, but any fast selloff can compress the view abruptly. Waiting for a nearer-to-settlement check would improve accuracy more than broader research would.
sections.Decision blockers: There is no major contract-language blocker; wording is clear. The real blockers are operational: single-minute settlement fragility, lack of direct capture of the governing candle at synthesis time, and uncertainty about whether the 0.71 market baseline was already stale. These do not require broad new research, just disciplined caution about edge size.
sections.Implication for the question: The best synthesis answer is still Yes-leaning: BTC was already trading materially above 70,000 and remained so in the synthesis-stage check, making a Yes resolution more likely than not. But because resolution depends on one exact Binance minute close, this should be treated as a high-probability intraday timing contract rather than a broad Bitcoin directional layup.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed that Polymarket’s source of truth is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close. All agreed BTC was already materially above 70,000 during research. All agreed the dominant residual risk was exact-minute path dependence rather than broad bearish fundamentals. All agreed source-of-truth ambiguity was low and that a sharp late selloff was the real failure mode.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was timing/weighting, not facts. Risk-manager was the most bullish numerically at 0.90 despite presenting a cautionary framing around timestamp fragility, which makes that lane somewhat overconfident relative to its own caveats. Base-rate and catalyst-hunter were more conservative around 0.78, assigning more mass to short-horizon downside tails. Market-implied and variant-view sat in between, with disagreement mostly about how much to trust the assignment baseline and how much to haircut exact-minute noise.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest surviving countercase is the risk-manager/variant-style caution: this contract can still resolve No on a brief late flush even if BTC trades above 70k for most of the morning. That countercase is substantive because the resolution object is one minute close, not daily direction.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance BTC/USDT is the operative settlement source; 12:00 ET maps to 16:00 UTC; pre-noon spot above 70k is informative; no venue anomaly occurs. Contested assumptions: how much short-horizon persistence should dominate versus crypto tail-risk; whether a stale assignment market meaningfully overstates the current edge. Fragile assumptions: that the recent above-threshold regime persists through the exact governing minute and that public API/chart alignment is operationally clean.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: explicit Polymarket rules, multiple direct Binance spot checks around 71.4k to 71.6k, recent Binance 1-minute candles above 70k, and a 24h low still above 70k in one lane’s check. Strongest contradictory evidence: BTC can move more than 2% intraday, and one bad minute is enough. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket rule text naming Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close. Ambiguous or mixed evidence: later live market pricing reportedly near 94% suggests the assignment baseline may be stale, which complicates edge assessment.
sections.Evidence weighting: I weighted explicit contract mechanics and direct Binance price checks most heavily. I downweighted any confidence claims that outran the evidence, especially the highest bullish estimate, because no one directly observed the governing candle. I also downweighted broad catalyst discussion because this market is mostly mechanical unless a concrete shock appears.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is mechanistic rather than narrative: Bitcoin does not need to trend down all day, it only needs one sufficiently sharp noon-minute downtick on Binance. That makes pre-settlement spot comfort less decisive than it would be for a daily close or end-of-day contract.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The contract appears operationally clear: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle at 12:00 ET on 2026-04-13 has a final close strictly above 70,000. Equality is insufficient. Source ambiguity is minor rather than none only because pre-settlement retrieval/alignment of the exact target candle can be operationally fussy, not because the wording itself is unclear.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Alpha exists only if the market is underweighting the probability that a pre-noon cushion above 70k persists into settlement. But this alpha can disappear quickly if the market already repriced from the assignment snapshot toward the mid-90s. The destroyer of alpha here is not hidden macro insight; it is stale baseline comparison and underestimated single-minute fragility.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A direct Binance read showing the exact 12:00 ET candle closed at or below 70,000 would fully falsify the Yes view. Before settlement, a sharp drop toward 70k, rising realized volatility, or evidence of Binance-specific dislocation would move the estimate materially lower. Conversely, a nearer-to-noon check still showing a comfortable cushion would push the view upward and narrow the range.
sections.Highest-value next research: The single highest-value next step is direct verification of the exact Binance 16:00 UTC / 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close. Nothing else is likely to move the estimate more.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class was strong: Polymarket rules for mechanics and Binance API/surfaces for the underlying price. The most important secondary class was cross-venue or live market context, which was helpful but not decisive. Evidence independence was medium at best because most useful evidence points back to the same settlement venue. Source-of-truth ambiguity was low. The synthesis is not bottlenecked by missing personas, but it is bottlenecked by the natural limitation of pre-settlement evidence in a single-minute contract.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, synthesis-stage verification was used. Cross-lane comparison materially reduced confidence in the swarm’s largest apparent edge by showing that most lanes were reusing the same core mechanism: price comfortably above strike plus little time remaining. The extra Binance check strengthened the Yes lean but also confirmed that the main remaining uncertainty had not changed: exact-minute path risk. The synthesis also exposed mild lane-level overconfidence in the most bullish estimate relative to its own caveats.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest concise outside-view persistence argument; useful because it framed the needed move as a >2% downside break in a short window. catalyst-hunter — useful narrowing of the question to absence of a negative shock rather than need for a positive catalyst. market-implied — best treatment of assignment baseline versus possible later market repricing; helpful for skepticism about stale prices. risk-manager — best articulation of timestamp/path-risk mechanics, though the final 0.90 estimate looked too aggressive relative to that caution. variant-view — best moderate bullish counter to excessive caution: above-threshold spot cushion matters, but should not be treated as decisive.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: in narrow intraday crypto threshold markets, the decisive object is often exact settlement mechanics rather than macro thesis. Possible underbuilt driver: explicit handling of stale market snapshots versus live market repricing in synthesis. Possible source-quality lesson: when the edge is large, demand direct capture of the exact target candle before endorsing a strong divergence. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: this case highlights a repeatable synthesis problem where a stale assignment market can create an illusory large edge and where lane confidence can outrun the evidence in minute-settlement contracts.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for and capture the exact Binance settlement candle, then update the downstream decision-maker with a post-settlement verification note. If a pre-settlement decision must be made, treat the edge as real but smaller and more fragile than the raw swarm numbers suggest.
